topics.title: Implicit Neural Representations: Fixed Positional Encoding vs Learnable Activations
topics.description: Debate on the best approach for INRs: fixed Fourier basis (Tancik et al.) vs learnable activations (SIREN)
research_gaps: How to handle non-bandlimited signals (natural images)?
What is the exact sample complexity for learnable activations?
How does dimension d affect different approaches?
Can we prove convergence rates for SIREN on realistic data?

topics.title: Implicit Neural Representations: Super-Resolution and Upsampling
topics.description: Can INRs solve super-resolution better than traditional methods? What are the theoretical limits?
foundational_papers: Sitzmann et al., 2022 - Signal Processing with INRs
Cohen & Welling, 2016 - Group Equivariance in CNNs
research_gaps: Can INRs recover frequencies beyond Nyquist limit?
How much training data is needed for different upsampling factors?
Do INRs generalize to unseen frequency content?

topics.title: Implicit Neural Representations: Efficiency vs Accuracy Trade-offs
topics.description: How should we trade off model size, training cost, and representation accuracy in INRs?
research_gaps: What is the minimal representation size for a given signal?
How does optimization difficulty scale with coordinate dimension?
Can we prove lower bounds on representation size?

topics.title: Neural ODEs vs Discrete Neural Networks: Function Approximation
topics.description: Are continuous Neural ODEs fundamentally better for approximating smooth functions?
foundational_papers: Chen et al., 2018 - Neural Ordinary Differential Equations
E, 2017 - A Proposal on Machine Learning via Dynamical Systems
Dupont et al., 2019 - Augmented Neural ODEs
research_gaps: What function classes require neural ODEs vs discrete networks?
Is continuous time strictly more expressive?
What are the approximation lower bounds?

topics.title: Transformer Attention: Self-Attention vs Cross-Attention for Representation Learning
topics.description: What are the theoretical advantages of different attention mechanisms?
foundational_papers: Vaswani et al., 2017 - Attention is All You Need
Pérez et al., 2019 - Attention is Not All You Need
Choromanski et al., 2021 - Rethinking Attention with Performers
research_gaps: What is the exact expressiveness of self-attention?
Can attention be replaced by simpler mechanisms for some tasks?

topics.title: Mode Connectivity in Neural Network Loss Landscapes: Theory vs Practice
topics.description: Why are neural networks trainable? Do all critical points connect in loss space?
foundational_papers: Garipov et al., 2018 - Loss Surfaces, Mode Connectivity
Jastrzębski et al., 2021 - Loss Landscape Structure
Keskar et al., 2017 - On Large-Batch Training
research_gaps: Do all local minima have similar generalization?
Can we characterize the global loss landscape?
